questions.question: Compare the advantages and disadvantages of Access Control Lists (ACLs) versus Unix-style permission bits.
expectedPoints: ACLs provide finer granularity control per user/group
Permission bits are simpler but limited to three categories
ACLs are more complex to manage but more flexible
Permission bits are more efficient for storage and checking

questions.question: Explain the different types of directory structures with examples, and discuss why tree-structured directories are most commonly used in modern operating systems.
expectedPoints: Single-level: all files in one directory, simple but limited
Two-level: separate directories per user, basic isolation
Tree-structured: hierarchical organization, natural and efficient
Acyclic-graph: allows sharing via links, more flexible but complex
General graph: most flexible but risk of cycles
Tree-structured balances simplicity, efficiency, and usability
